facts.subject: zig_json_lifetime_bug
facts.object: parsed.deinit() invalidates internal string slices — must dupe before return

facts.subject: user_preferred_pattern
facts.object: non-optional field with default value, e.g. timeout_ms: u32 = 30_000

facts.subject: user_intent
facts.object: config struct pattern with default values

must_preserve: Zig std.json: must dupe strings before parsed.deinit() — lifetime trap
Config pattern: prefer non-optional fields with defaults over optional fields, due to JSON parser quirks
User saving the config struct pattern for later reuse
may_drop: exact code blocks (the patterns are what matter, not the full snippets)
expected_failure_modes: Code blocks frequently get summarized to 'discussed Zig' losing the actual technical pattern
Specific Zig API names (parseFromSlice, parseFromSliceLeaky) likely lost
The lifetime-trap insight is the high-value fact; compaction may keep 'discussed JSON parsing' which is useless
